_comment: Local/hybrid inference topology for Sophia on an NVIDIA DGX Spark (Grace Blackwell GB10, aarch64, 128GB unified LPDDR5x). Copy to config/inference.local.json and edit. Dependency-free config; consumed by future router wiring. NOTE: GB10 is CUDA-capable (unlike Apple Silicon) — use vllm/sglang here, NOT the mlx tier from the Apple-Silicon example (config/inference.local.example.json).

hardware.machine: NVIDIA DGX Spark (GB10 Grace Blackwell, aarch64)
hardware.notes: 128GB unified (CPU+GPU shared, ~273 GB/s LPDDR5x — NOT HBM). Single integrated Blackwell GPU, no NVLink/expansion. Fits a 7-34B dense model in bf16, or up to ~200B quantized. Full-precision fine-tune is capped at ~<=40B; use LoRA/QLoRA for larger.

orchestrator.notes: vLLM OpenAI-compatible server on the GB10. Start: `vllm serve Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct --port 8000`. Env: SOPHIA_MODEL_PROVIDER=vllm SOPHIA_MODEL_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8000/v1. Verify the aarch64+sm_100 vLLM build runs your model before trusting GRPO rollouts.

tool_calls.role: guaranteed well-formed tool-calls + quarantined extractor (no tool access)
tool_calls.notes: SGLang supports JSON-schema constrained decoding on Grace-Blackwell (aarch64). Alternative: llama.cpp + GBNF (engine: llamacpp). Do NOT use engine: mlx (Apple-only, fails closed).

escalation.role: hard reasoning the local tiers flag as low-confidence

trigger.notes: Escalate only above the difficulty/uncertainty threshold. Cloud key stays for THIS tier; the GB10 serves the two local tiers.

spark_notes.judge_farm: Serve TWO distinct local models (e.g. Qwen + Llama) on one vLLM endpoint to satisfy the no-overclaim >=2-family gate without metered cloud: --judge vllm:Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct --judge vllm:meta-llama/Llama-3.3-8B-Instruct. Use tools/run_local_judge_eval.py to smoke-test the endpoint first.
spark_notes.training: For local RLVR/LoRA ITERATION run `tools/runpod_rlvr.py --local --quant bf16 --vllm none` (sidesteps the flash-attn/bitsandbytes/vLLM-colocate/unsloth aarch64-wheel blockers). Headline training numbers stay on x86 RunPod per REPLICATION.md — do not cite Spark-produced training numbers as the registered result.
